sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Abdominal pain.
History of Present Illness
Barbara reports one-week onset of abdominal pain, attributing it to increased coffee consumption and possibly related to recent work-out sessions. She describes the pain as gradually increasing in severity. She reports intermittent nausea and has vomited twice this week. She has been drinking a lot of coffee, including Starbucks nitro drinks, and reports a history of alcohol use. She believes the coffee intake may be related to the abdominal pain.
Findings
Physical exam reveals mild epigastric tenderness. The patient's heart exam is notable for a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, which is unchanged from the prior exam. Lung exam reveals mild end-expiratory wheezes. Otherwise, the physical exam is unremarkable.
Assessment
Acute gastritis is likely, possibly related to caffeine intake and alcohol use.
Plan
Prescribe zantac for twice daily administration. Order urinalysis, urine pregnancy test, and CBC with CMP. Increase metformin dosage to 1000 mg in the morning and 500 mg in the evening. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks. Advise the patient to reduce caffeine and alcohol intake. Recommend limiting spicy foods.
